_info._purpose: This file controls duplicate detection tolerances for the BDO Loot Tracker. Adjust these values to improve tracking accuracy without changing code.
_info._tolerance_format: [y_tolerance, x_tolerance] - Maximum pixel distance in Y and X directions to consider an item a duplicate
_info._how_it_works: When an item is detected, the tracker checks if a similar item was recently detected within these pixel distances. If yes, it's considered a duplicate and not counted again.

_accuracy_tuning_guide.overcounting_issue: If you track MORE than you actually loot, TIGHTEN the tolerance (reduce numbers). This prevents false duplicates from being counted as separate items.
_accuracy_tuning_guide.undercounting_issue: If you track LESS than you actually loot, LOOSEN the tolerance (increase numbers). This allows more variation in item position before being considered a duplicate.
_accuracy_tuning_guide.perfect_accuracy: When tracked count matches looted count, the tolerance is optimal for that item.

_category_based_defaults.trash: [25, 35] - Loose tolerance for common trash items that appear frequently and may shift position
_category_based_defaults.General: [15, 25] - Medium tolerance for general items like black stones
_category_based_defaults.rare: [12, 18] - Tight tolerance for rare items to prevent false positives
_category_based_defaults.default: [12, 18] - Fallback for items without a category

_info._manual_overrides_section: Use _manual_overrides to fine-tune specific items. Overrides take precedence over auto-generated values.

_testing_workflow.step_1: Grind for 5-10 minutes and record tracked vs looted counts
_testing_workflow.step_2: Identify items with accuracy issues (overcounting or undercounting)
_testing_workflow.step_3: For overcounting items: Add to _manual_overrides with TIGHTER tolerance (smaller numbers)
_testing_workflow.step_4: For undercounting items: Add to _manual_overrides with LOOSER tolerance (larger numbers)
_testing_workflow.step_5: Test again and iterate until accuracy is 95%+ for all items
_testing_workflow.step_6: Share your optimized _manual_overrides with the community

_machine_learning_integration.suggested_approach: Use accuracy data (tracked vs looted) to train a model that predicts optimal tolerances based on item properties (category, price, drop rate, name length, etc.)
_machine_learning_integration.training_data: Collect grinding sessions with known ground truth (actual looted counts) to train the model

_community_collaboration.share_optimized_tolerances: Post your _manual_overrides section to forums/discord with your accuracy results
_community_collaboration.regional_differences: Note that optimal tolerances may vary by screen resolution, UI scale, and game client version
_community_collaboration.version_control: Keep backups of working tolerance configs before experimenting

_notes.item_names: All item names must be lowercase to match the tracker's internal format
_notes.tolerance_ranges: Recommended range: y_tolerance [8-40], x_tolerance [12-50]. Values outside this range may cause issues.
_notes.special_cases: Items with very similar names or icons may need tighter tolerances to prevent misidentification.
_notes.resolution_scaling: Higher screen resolutions may require proportionally larger tolerances.
